claim: Verified: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation exists with git-backed and non-git fallback coverage. Stress testing with real Claude -p agents is the primary gap. All assumptions are clearly separated from facts.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git ls-files output with walkFiles() fallback and excludes allowedRoots.
evidence.why: Verifies implementation matches acceptance criteria for boundary coverage.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs git ls-files -co --exclude-standard to capture tracked, cached, and untracked files.
evidence.why: Confirms git-backed mutation detection is implemented as required.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() provides recursive directory fallback excluding common build artifacts.
evidence.why: Verifies non-git fallback coverage exists for non-git projects.

evidence.summary: Tests cover git-backed detection of untracked files and gitignored mutations.
evidence.why: Test evidence supports git-backed mutation detection claims.

evidence.summary: Test verifies non-git fallback walkFiles and artifact exclusion.
evidence.why: Test evidence supports fallback coverage claims.

evidence.summary: Test suite uses fakeAgentOutput mocks rather than real Claude -p provider invocations.
evidence.why: Confirms stress testing gap is factual, not assumed.

evidence.summary: Git status shows 65 modified files and untracked .yolo/demand/ and .yolo/stress/ directories.
evidence.why: Verifies dirty working tree claim is factual.

evidence.summary: .yolo/stress/ directory exists but contains no evidence files.
evidence.why: Supports claim that stress testing is incomplete.

assumptions: Explorer assumption that .yolo/stress/ directory indicates stress testing intent is reasonable given context but unverified by documentation.
Explorer assumption that tests using mock agents means real agent behavior is untested is logical but not explicitly confirmed by project docs.
risks: If git ls-files behavior differs across git versions, boundary detection may be inconsistent.
If real Claude -p agents use tool access patterns not anticipated by test mocks, boundary violations could occur undetected.
If file walk fallback timeout behavior differs on large projects, boundary audit may fail silently.

result.notes: Explorer evidence is thorough and properly scoped. All project facts are backed by code, tests, or git state with exact paths. Assumptions are clearly identified and separated from verified facts. The primary gap (stress testing with real Claude -p agents) is accurately reflected in missing data rather than promoted to a false completion status. Evidence paths are usable and support PRD readiness verification.
